body: Re-investigated the Terminal-closing mystery (2026-07-18 attempts) with available event-log forensics. Honest null result, not a fabricated answer: (1) an early lead -- a Windows RestartManager session ending ~5min before Attempt 1's close -- turned out NON-REPRODUCIBLE on requery (found once via Get-WinEvent, then zero times on 3 identical retries), so I'm retracting it rather than treat it as evidence; this also means Get-WinEvent query results may not be fully reliable in this environment for future forensic work, worth knowing. (2) A real, wevtutil-confirmed finding: the machine had a sleep/resume cycle with a ~10.5hr clock correction at 11:50 local that day -- but it's 57min before Attempt 1's close and 4+ hours from Attempt 2's, too loose to call causal. (3) Zero terminal.exe/MetaTrader/Avira mentions anywhere in System or Application logs at either close timestamp. I don't have a new answer -- this remains genuinely unexplained by what I can check from here. Per your own 'no retries or crashes ever' instruction, I'm not attempting a third live launch to test it. Two questions only you can answer would actually move this forward: were you closing the terminal yourself either time (Alt+F4, taskbar, habit), or is there other local software on this machine that might be doing it? Everything else from this session (WARRANT#80-85, SLS(85)->SLS(90)/10.04) is unaffected and already sent separately.
